headlines.title: Thousands of Kurds gather in Turkiye to demand release of PKK leader
headlines.snippet: Thousands of Kurds in Turkiye rallied to demand the release of PKK leader Abdullah Öcalan and other prisoners.

headlines.title: Oil prices rise as US, Iranian strikes threaten Strait of Hormuz reopening
headlines.snippet: Oil prices increased as strikes between the United States and Iran created concerns about the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. Brent crude saw a slight rise, with events indicating a return to normalcy in the key waterway following tit-for-tat strikes.

headlines.title: Australian man charged with murder after Thai girl’s body found in suitcase
headlines.snippet: An Australian man, Simon Peter Carman, has been charged with murder following the discovery of the body of 17-year-old Tunchanok Donhomla in a suitcase. The circumstances surrounding the discovery are part of an ongoing investigation.

headlines.title: Venezuelans angry with government as time runs out to rescue survivors
headlines.snippet: Venezuelan residents and volunteers express anger and a feeling of abandonment by the government as they race against time to rescue survivors from earthquake rubble. The government's response is under scrutiny amid the ongoing crisis.

headlines.title: Putin says Ukraine proposed halt to deep strikes
headlines.snippet: Russian President Vladimir Putin stated that Ukraine has proposed a mutual cessation of long-range strikes. This proposal indicates a potential de-escalation in the ongoing conflict between the two nations.

headlines.title: US and Iran to discuss Hormuz tensions in Qatar, says report
headlines.snippet: US and Iran to discuss Hormuz tensions in Qatar, says report The United States and Iran are expected to hold direct talks in Doha on Tuesday aimed at resolving disputes over security and navigation arrangements in the Strait of Hormuz, according to Axios, which cited US officials.

headlines.title: New Jersey governor says Trump has not achieved Iran war aims

headlines.title: Data shows 48 vessels crossed Hormuz after attacks
headlines.snippet: Data indicates that 48 vessels transited the Strait of Hormuz between June 26 and 28, following recent strikes between the United States and Iran. This figure represents a decrease compared to previous days, suggesting potential impacts on maritime traffic due to security concerns.

headlines.title: Iranian-American lawmaker says Trump’s comments on Iran are ‘unhinged’
headlines.snippet: US Representative Yassamin Ansari, an Iranian-American Democrat from Arizona, has criticized President Donald Trump's recent statements regarding Iran. She described his rhetoric as "dangerous" and "unhinged," responding to a post threatening military action.
